description_long: This is a rustic loaf of bread with a thick, uneven crust and a natural, hand-formed appearance. The surface is mottled with patches of light flour dusting and darker, baked areas, giving it a hearty, artisanal look. The loaf is oblong and slightly lumpy, with irregular folds and creases along its sides. The crust appears firm and textured, with some areas showing deeper browning and others lighter, almost golden tones. There are no visible slices or cuts, and the bread looks whole and unsliced. The overall impression is of a traditional, country-style bread, likely baked in a stone oven.
description: A rustic, unsliced loaf of bread with a thick, mottled crust and flour-dusted surface. Its oblong shape and uneven texture give it an artisanal, hand-formed appearance.
description_view_0: This view shows the loaf's broad side, highlighting the mottled crust and flour patches with a prominent crease running along the top.
description_view_1: The end of the loaf is visible here, showing a rounded, slightly indented area and more pronounced flour dusting on the crust.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the loaf's oblong shape and the irregular, rustic folds along the crust.
description_view_3: The opposite end of the loaf is shown, with a smoother, less indented surface and a more even distribution of crust color.
attribute_crust_pattern: The crust has a rustic, uneven pattern with irregular flour dusting and natural folds. There are no visible scores or seeds, and the surface is mottled with darker baked areas and lighter patches.
